item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.3 No.8, 1955
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE No. 8 (44) Vol. III 1955
UNIVERSITY OF UTAH LIBRARIES
COSTUMES OF BRITAIN IN THE MID-XXth CENTURY
444 NEW EDWARDIAN STYLES
Never before in the long story of male attire has there been so obvious an attempt to wear the clothes of a previous generation...
445 SURPLUS STORES STYLES
For so long men's clothes have been deliberately uncomfortable, as if to show that their wearers did not intend, and had no need, to soil their hands with work...
446 IMMIGREE STYLES
They have, so to speak, been through European clothes, and come out on the other side into a world of freedom and fantasy...
447 HOLIDAY STYLES
We have at last almost arrived at the logical end of bathing costumes with the invention of the Bikini...
These four "fashion plates" are interpretations and not, as always in the CHRONICLE, immediate drawings "from life".
Nos. 445—447 are reproduced by permission of the proprietors of PUNCH; quotations are taken from James Laver's articles in PUNCH.
pages.handwritten_text:
long hair - not brushed
very high collar
short tie, soft hat
shiny cuffs, slightly fancy shoes
Edwardian embroidery
Duffle coats
waterproof
labels
old Army boots
paratroop jackets
shabby raincoat
suit
French hat
Jewish trousers
painted shoes
Summer hats
Bikini top
loose wrap
sunburnt
cane
sandals
sketch_descriptions: A group of men in 'New Edwardian Styles,' including bowler hats, fitted coats, and distinct period details, standing together in conversation.
Figures in 'Surplus Stores Styles,' wearing duffle coats, army surplus clothing, boots, and raincoats, suggesting practicality and repurposing of attire.
A group of people, some in wide-brimmed hats and trench coats, labeled as 'Immigree Styles,' with expressive and flamboyant outfits reflecting European influence and individuality.
Two figures in 'Holiday Styles,' one elderly man with cane and sandals and another in a bikini, highlighting the evolution and lightening of bathing costumes.
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle visually explores four distinct mid-20th century British costume styles—Edwardian revival, surplus store clothing, immigrant-influenced fashion, and holiday beachwear—combining Topolski's expressive sketches and handwritten notes with editorial commentary.

pages.typeset_text:
COSTUMES OF THE BRITISH MALE IN THE MID-XXth CENTURY
Old and New Edwardians, Teddy Boys and the Others
Drawings in this issue are available as postcards (single and in series of 15) and as greeting cards (harmonicas: made of 7 drawings). Some originals can also be had.
pages.handwritten_text:
HANDWRITTEN TEXTS BELOW IMAGES:
- GUARDS’ OFFICERS’ STYLES
- UNDERGRADUATE STYLES
- CHELSEA STYLES
- “TEDDY BOY” STYLES (appears twice)
- CHARING STYLES
- “AT LORDS” STYLES
- WORKMEN STYLES
- VAGABOND STYLES
- WEST INDIAN STYLES

Some cursive notations within the sketches, e.g., “Oxford”, “Chelsea”, “London”, “‘Teddy Boy’” and possibly artist’s notes on a few images that are partially unclear.
sketch_descriptions: Group of men in formal uniforms and hats, with upright posture suggesting officers in a social setting.
Two young men standing together, one tall and slender with a long coat, evoking a university context.
Scene of casually dressed men and women in a lively urban environment, possibly an art district.
Two young men in stylish, sharp suits known as 'Teddy Boy' attire, with one woman in conversation.
Solitary figure in profile with curled hair, dressed in 'Teddy Boy' style.
Crowds moving through or congregating near a monument reminiscent of Trafalgar Square with Nelson's Column.
Men in light summer suits and hats walking at a venue labeled as 'At Lords', recalling a cricket match.
Two workmen in rough, layered attire carrying bags and everyday items.
Disheveled individual in oversized clothes, hunched and carrying bags, showing the 'Vagabond' style.
Pair of men in wide-brimmed hats and loose suits, labeled as 'West Indian Styles', walking together.
pages.page_summary: This page presents a series of expressive sketches by Felix Topolski depicting a diverse range of British male fashion styles in the mid-20th century, from formal and subcultural to working-class and immigrant influences. The images are accompanied by handwritten captions categorizing each style and highlight the social and sartorial variety in postwar Britain.

pages.typeset_text:
"TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR AT 2s. 6d. A COPY
YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES (WITH FOLDER AND INDEX) TO ANY PART OF THE WORLD ARE £3. 3s. ($9 or equivalents); SHORTER PERIODS PRO RATA, AIR MAIL BY QUOTATION.
SUBSCRIPTIONS FROM OUTSIDE THE STERLING AREA MAY BE PAID THROUGH THE UNESCO COUPON SCHEME
Handprinted in Great Britain by Poets' & Painters' Press 146 Sutton Walk, S.E.1. from blocks made by Austin Miles Ltd and published from Bridge Arch 156, Sutton Walk, South Bank, London, S.E.1. Copyright reserved.
sketch_descriptions: A lively, dense street scene dominated by the sketch of a woman walking in the foreground, surrounded by several adults and children.
In the background, a two-story building with a sign reading 'FITHS DAIRIES' and a partially obscured street setting.
Groups of people are gathered under and around the shopfront, with expressive lines suggesting movement and urban bustle.
pages.page_summary: This page captures a bustling street scene in London's East End, featuring expressive sketches of pedestrians gathered near a local dairy and annotated with Topolski's location. It combines urban architecture with dynamic human figures, illustrating city life.
